published_prior_art.cite: AbadieDiamondHainmueller2010 + BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 + ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021 + AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021: exact-balance/common-corner ingredients are known, but not packaged as a four-node lattice sanity theorem

repo_prior_art.cite: Q1_MinimalBasis/Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis/Q1_SpectralThreshold are regression-history/FWL spectral entries, not convex-SCM/ASCM/SDiD/MC functional-domain lattice results

published_prior_art.cite: BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021: ASCM paper treats SDiD as a special case of its augmented/outcome-regression proposal; full four-node strict lattice and minimal witnesses still not found

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 comparison is too weak: the paper/working-paper text says SDiD can be viewed as a special case of their augmented/outcome-regression proposal, so the proposed affine--SDiD strict-inclusion direction needs a sharper distinction from that prior relationship.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The matrix-completion functional is not a deterministic functional as written: the nuclear-norm/rank-constrained program has no global tie-breaking rule, and its penalized observed-entry fit is not the same convention as the exact-completion class \calM_R(P_obs).

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed affine/ridge ASCM subset SDiD lattice is not supported by the setup: ASCM uses signed affine weights while SDiD keeps simplex unit weights plus time weights, and no explicit embedding or domain-order definition makes these coefficient spaces nested.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the affine--SDiD inclusion claim by an explicit equality-frontier statement that distinguishes this affine/ridge node from the augmented-outcome-regression class in BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021.
Make \phi_mc a true single-valued population functional: either constrain observed entries exactly and add deterministic tie-breaking, or state a set-valued MC node and redefine \calF_mc accordingly.
Add a boundary corollary showing when the proposal reproduces the published SDiD-as-constrained-augmentation relationship, then state precisely what extra Schur/Riesz certificate is new.

recommended_next_step: Revise the ASCM--SDiD prior-art comparison and repair the MC functional definition before sending this to Stage 0.
